title: The List

jokes.text: Planking is one of those things where, hey, you either get it or you don't. And I don't. But I am so excited to be a part of it.
jokes.setup: Character explains planking as stupid and dangerous
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't get it but is excited to participate anyway
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic logic that captures modern social media participation - well-structured contradiction

jokes.text: He drove down to Florida and convinced Jo to make him CEO. CEO... Her own job. He talked her out of her own job and I don't really know how someone does that.
jokes.setup: Robert California was supposed to be manager
jokes.punchline: Instead talked the CEO out of her own job to become CEO himself
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that establishes Robert California's manipulative power - the repetition and bewilderment sell it

jokes.text: True, I might have been the second choice, but I was the first choice's first choice.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to justify his manager position
jokes.punchline: Convoluted logic to make being second choice sound better
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - over-explaining to make himself feel better, economically phrased

jokes.text: This has got to stop. - I can't get down. Ke-- Kevin!
jokes.setup: Andy demanding planking stop
jokes.punchline: Kevin is stuck planking and can't get down
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the planking setup - Kevin being stuck is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I've upped my karate to eight times a week. I've added boxing, lunches and on weekends. I do kickboxing three times a week, krav maga four times a week, an hour of meditation every morning at sunrise, and again at sunset.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying he's doing great after not getting the job
jokes.punchline: Absurdly excessive workout schedule that's mathematically impossible
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight - channeling disappointment into over-the-top physical training, escalating absurdly

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, pregnant. Right here. Little Michael Scott. - Nope. I told you I don't like that joke.
jokes.setup: Jim realizes Pam is pregnant and can't help with planking
jokes.punchline: Makes a callback joke about naming baby Michael Scott that Pam hates
jokes.explanation: Callback that works through Pam's annoyed reaction - shows ongoing couple dynamic

jokes.text: Look! It's, uh, little pregs and big pregs.
jokes.setup: Two pregnant women in the office
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate nickname system
jokes.explanation: Peak Kevin inappropriateness - economical and perfectly in character

jokes.text: I'm having a child with my husband the senator, and Pam is having a child with Jim... The great salesman.
jokes.setup: Angela comparing her pregnancy situation to Pam's
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes 'the senator' vs dismissive 'the great salesman'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela condescension - the pause and tone shift makes the put-down clear

jokes.text: It might be easier if you take a deep breath, lift from the knees, and shove it up your butt.
jokes.setup: Sounds like helpful lifting advice
jokes.punchline: Ends with 'shove it up your butt'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sounds genuinely helpful before the punchline lands

jokes.text: I came up with a new thing this summer. I act like I'm telling someone how to do something. I go on with a long description and then I say, 'and shove it up your butt!' It's stupid, but it's my thing now.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his joke formula
jokes.punchline: Self-aware that it's stupid but owns it anyway
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary that's perfect for Stanley - acknowledging the stupidity while committing to it

jokes.text: Kids, don't try planking. It's dangerous. Especially with me around.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving safety advice about planking
jokes.punchline: Adds threatening qualifier about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - turns safety PSA into vague threat, economically delivered

jokes.text: The new CEO works out of the conference room about half the time. But whenever he takes a break, he does these weird walks around the office. And you never know who he's going to zero in on for these really intense small talks. You just hope it's not you. And yet you hope it is you too. It's strange.
jokes.setup: Describing Robert California's office behavior
jokes.punchline: The contradictory feelings about wanting and not wanting his attention
jokes.explanation: Captures the weird dynamic with intimidating authority figures - relatable psychological insight

jokes.text: Erin, when you recount your day, never say you woke up, it's a waste of your time. That's how every day has begun for everyone since the dawn of man. - Very smart. Very smart. Suddenly... I was awake.
jokes.setup: Robert gives pretentious advice about storytelling
jokes.punchline: Erin immediately adopts his advice with childlike literalness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character collision - Robert's pretension meets Erin's eagerness to please

jokes.text: First item on the agenda-- Can I get everyone an extra long Columbus day weekend? Item number two-- Connect with the guy. Robert California-- What does he think of me? Don't know. Super care. Number three, time permitting, we lost our biggest client.
jokes.setup: Andy listing meeting agenda items
jokes.punchline: Priorities go from weekend request to needing approval to major crisis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Andy's misplaced priorities - builds to the biggest problem being lowest priority

jokes.text: Kind of a medium year for women's soccer, no?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to make small talk with Robert
jokes.punchline: Completely random, awkward topic choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward Andy small talk - completely out of nowhere and trying too hard

jokes.text: We don't need a warning signal, Kevin. I can see him right there. We do! - I promise you, we don't need-- - Warning! Warning! Warning! Warning!
jokes.setup: Jim insisting they don't need a warning signal
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately starts shouting warnings anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing - Kevin's panic overrides logic despite Jim's reasonable point

jokes.text: Really great list of names, guys. Thank you so much. Good work.
jokes.setup: Employees caught looking at his private notebook
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically congratulates them on their snooping
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan sarcasm - treats their invasion of privacy like a work accomplishment

jokes.text: This is if we were all on a cruise ship and had to divide into lifeboats. And this is if we were on a cruise ship and had to divide into life rafts. Here's something. Who would eat who in alive situation.
jokes.setup: Dwight showing his old lists to compare
jokes.punchline: Escalates from practical to cannibalistic scenarios
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - practical disaster planning that escalates to disturbing extremes, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Don't be such a right-sider.
jokes.setup: Pam defending the right side of the list
jokes.punchline: Oscar creates a dismissive term for her list position
jokes.explanation: Economical wordplay that turns list position into personality flaw

jokes.text: Some people doodle at work when they let their mind run. They draw houses, penises... Funny how the houses are always colonials, and the penises are always circumcised, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Robert explaining that his list is just a doodle
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific observations about doodle patterns
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Robert California observation - unexpectedly specific and inappropriate for the workplace

jokes.text: Well, you take the first letter from each name, assign it a number, add 'em all up, and shove it up your butt!
jokes.setup: Stanley appears to be giving genuine puzzle-solving advice
jokes.punchline: Ends with his recurring 'shove it up your butt' punchline
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that maintains the misdirection structure while being expected

jokes.text: Attack! Wait, wait, wait. Stop, stop, stop. Will you stop? Dwight! Dwight! Go for it!
jokes.setup: Andy trying to organize the groups for comparison
jokes.punchline: Chaos ensues with conflicting commands to attack and stop
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy chaos showing Andy's inability to maintain control

jokes.text: Pizza party! ♪ Pizza party ♪ ♪ pizza party... ♪
jokes.setup: Erin trying to make the 'losers' feel better about their consolation pizza
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic song that highlights how sad their situation is
jokes.explanation: Erin's forced enthusiasm makes the consolation prize feel even more pathetic

jokes.text: Ours is a cultural ghetto. Wouldn't you agree? - Yeah. She does like elmo. - 'Cultural ghetto'? Totally, totally agree. - Completely. - Apt. Apt analysis, Robert.
jokes.setup: Robert makes pretentious observation about Elmo and society
jokes.punchline: Everyone desperately agrees without understanding, Jim gives literal response
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - shows everyone's desperation to impress Robert with fake intellectual agreement

jokes.text: The thing that I like about elmo is the tickling. - I should not be here. I'm in the-- I was in the wrong-- I'm...I'm sorry. Uh, just picture me back there. I-I was never here.
jokes.setup: Kevin makes inappropriate comment about Elmo tickling
jokes.punchline: Toby realizes he's in the wrong group and awkwardly tries to disappear
jokes.explanation: Double cringe - Kevin's inappropriate comment followed by Toby's mortified retreat

jokes.text: Well, why don't you ask me again when the five pizzas get here? - Yeah, well that's just pizza. You need at least one other element for it to be a party.
jokes.setup: Erin defending calling it a pizza party
jokes.punchline: Oscar's logical deconstruction of what constitutes a party
jokes.explanation: Oscar's pedantic precision deflating Erin's forced enthusiasm

jokes.text: I guess I think they're losers. - Ah, I knew it. Yes! - Probably shouldn't have said that. - Whoo! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Robert reluctantly admits his honest opinion
jokes.punchline: Winners celebrate while Robert immediately regrets his honesty
jokes.explanation: Robert's rare moment of unfiltered honesty creates immediate awkwardness and celebration

jokes.text: 'Suck it, losers.'
jokes.setup: Kevin texting the losers group
jokes.punchline: Blunt, mean message delivered through Pam reading it
jokes.explanation: Peak Kevin cruelty - economically mean and perfectly in character

jokes.text: I used to be young and cute and sort of funny, and... I could do those cute, little cartoons. And everyone who came through here was like... 'Who's that receptionist? I like her.' Now I'm just a fat mom. And you take one look at me and you're like, 'oh, loser.'
jokes.setup: Pam reflecting on being categorized as a loser
jokes.punchline: Brutal self-assessment about her transformation from cute receptionist to 'fat mom'
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking character moment that's funny through its brutal honesty and self-awareness

jokes.text: Chins up, okay? - Bad joke.
jokes.setup: Erin trying to encourage Pam
jokes.punchline: Realizes she made an insensitive pun about chins to someone feeling fat
jokes.explanation: Perfect accidental insensitivity followed by immediate self-awareness

jokes.text: Just-- just take the-- take the casserole out of the-- Loser. Take it out of the refrigerator and put it in the oven. Uh, it'll be fine. Just leave it in for 20 minutes. Loser. Losers.
jokes.setup: Jim giving Pam cooking instructions over the phone
jokes.punchline: Unconsciously calling her 'loser' repeatedly while talking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychological comedy - the label has infected his unconscious speech patterns

jokes.text: When I was a salesman, I could just be like, 'not my job, not my prob. I'm going to the warehouse to Polish my knob.' Metaphorically, of course.
jokes.setup: Andy missing the freedom of not being manager
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate innuendo followed by clarification it's metaphorical
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo that Andy immediately tries to backpedal from

jokes.text: Constantly worrying about where you stand based on inscrutable social clues, and then inevitably reframing it all in a reassuring way so that you can get to sleep at night. No, I do not believe in that at all.
jokes.setup: Robert explaining what he finds unproductive
jokes.punchline: Perfectly describes exactly what everyone in the office is doing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary - Robert unknowingly describes the entire premise of workplace anxiety

jokes.text: Stanley-- You may think he's a lazy grump, but did you know that he has the most consistently high sales numbers of anyone in this office? And you may think he's hard to love, but did you know that he's in, not one, but two long-term romantic relationships?
jokes.setup: Andy defending Stanley to Robert
jokes.punchline: The defense accidentally confirms Stanley is a lazy grump and hard to love
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured defense that undermines itself - Andy admits the negative while defending

jokes.text: Meredith Palmer, supplier relations-- The word 'no,' not even in her vocabulary.
jokes.setup: Andy praising Meredith's work ethic
jokes.punchline: Phrasing suggests sexual promiscuity rather than professional dedication
jokes.explanation: Double meaning that works on professional and personal levels for Meredith

jokes.text: And you are aware that Columbus and his legions committed genocide against an entire civilization of native Americans. - I don't care.
jokes.setup: Robert bringing up the dark history of Columbus Day
jokes.punchline: Andy's blunt dismissal of genocide for a long weekend
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark - Andy's casual dismissal of historical atrocities for vacation time is shockingly funny

jokes.text: I mean, maybe it's stupid. - No. It's wonderful.
jokes.setup: Pam showing Jim a drawing and being self-deprecating
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate, sincere contradiction
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Jim immediately supporting Pam's artistic confidence

tmdb_overview: Robert California arrives as CEO and leaves a mysterious list dividing the office staff.
